title: Padma Shri

infobox.value: [State Emblem of India](/wiki/File:Emblem_of_India.svg "State Emblem of India") [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India")
A centrally located lotus flower is embossed and the text "Padma" written in Devanagari script is placed above and the text "Shri" is placed below the lotus.

A platinum [State Emblem of India](/wiki/State_Emblem_of_India "State Emblem of India") placed in the centre with the national motto of India, "[Satyameva Jayate](/wiki/Satyameva_Jayate "Satyameva Jayate")" (Truth alone triumphs) in Devanagari Script

([IAST](/wiki/IAST "IAST"): *padma śrī*, lit. 'Lotus Honour'), also spelled **Padma Shree**, is the fourth-highest [civilian award](/wiki/Indian_honours_system "Indian honours system") of the [Republic of India](/wiki/Republic_of_India "Republic of India"), after the [Bharat Ratna](/wiki/Bharat_Ratna "Bharat Ratna"), the [Padma Vibhushan](/wiki/Padma_Vibhushan "Padma Vibhushan") and the [Padma Bhushan](/wiki/Padma_Bhushan "Padma Bhushan"). Instituted on 2 January 1954, the award is conferred in recognition of "distinguished contribution in various spheres of activity including the arts, education, industry, literature, science, acting, medicine, social service and public affairs". It is awarded by the [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") every year on [India's Republic Day](/wiki/Republic_Day_(India) "Republic Day (India)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
["Padma award's schema"](http://mha.nic.in/sites/upload_files/mha/files/Scheme-PadmaAwards-050514.pdf) (PDF). Ministry of Home Affairs. Retrieved 13 August 2014.
["Padma's Easy Slim Zone | Vrinda Gopinath"](http://www.outlookindia.com/article.aspx?283756). Outlookindia.com. Retrieved 10 July 2013.

["Advani backs Merchant on Padma awards selection criticism"](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/more-sports/snooker/billiards/Advani-backs-Merchant-on-Padma-awards-selection-criticism/tpoint_articleshow/5531225.cms). *The Times of India*. 3 February 2010. Retrieved 1 November 2017.

["Padma Awards Online Nomination"](https://web.archive.org/web/20180916164054/https://www.padmaawards.gov.in/SelectionGuidelines.aspx). *padmaawards.gov.in*. Archived from [the original](https://www.padmaawards.gov.in/SelectionGuidelines.aspx) on 16 September 2018. Retrieved 28 April 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Fourth highest civilian award of India

Award

| Padma Shri | |
| --- | --- |
| [Padma Shri medal suspended from its riband](/wiki/File:Padma_Shri_India_IIIe_Klasse.jpg) | |
| Type | National Civilian |
| Country | [India](/wiki/India "India") |
| Presented by | [State Emblem of India](/wiki/File:Emblem_of_India.svg "State Emblem of India") [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") |
| Ribbon |  |
| Obverse | A centrally located lotus flower is embossed and the text "Padma" written in Devanagari script is placed above and the text "Shri" is placed below the lotus. |
| Reverse | A platinum [State Emblem of India](/wiki/State_Emblem_of_India "State Emblem of India") placed in the centre with the national motto of India, "[Satyameva Jayate](/wiki/Satyameva_Jayate "Satyameva Jayate")" (Truth alone triumphs) in Devanagari Script |

The **Padma Shri** ([IAST](/wiki/IAST "IAST"): *padma śrī*, lit. 'Lotus Honour'), also spelled **Padma Shree**, is the fourth-highest [civilian award](/wiki/Indian_honours_system "Indian honours system") of the [Republic of India](/wiki/Republic_of_India "Republic of India"), after the [Bharat Ratna](/wiki/Bharat_Ratna "Bharat Ratna"), the [Padma Vibhushan](/wiki/Padma_Vibhushan "Padma Vibhushan") and the [Padma Bhushan](/wiki/Padma_Bhushan "Padma Bhushan"). Instituted on 2 January 1954, the award is conferred in recognition of "distinguished contribution in various spheres of activity including the arts, education, industry, literature, science, acting, medicine, social service and public affairs". It is awarded by the [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") every year on [India's Republic Day](/wiki/Republic_Day_(India) "Republic Day (India)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Padma_Shri&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/83/Padmasree_award_certificate.jpeg/250px-Padmasree_award_certificate.jpeg)](/wiki/File:Padmasree_award_certificate.jpeg)

Padma Shri certificate during the presidency of [K. R. Narayanan](/wiki/K._R._Narayanan "K. R. Narayanan") awarded to Telugu journalist [Turlapaty Kutumba Rao](/wiki/Turlapaty_Kutumba_Rao "Turlapaty Kutumba Rao")

Padma Awards were instituted in 1954 to be awarded to citizens of India in recognition of their distinguished contribution in various spheres of activity including the [arts](/wiki/Arts "Arts"), [education](/wiki/Education "Education"), [industry](/wiki/Private_industry "Private industry"), [literature](/wiki/Literature "Literature"), [science](/wiki/Science "Science"), [acting](/wiki/Acting "Acting"), [medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine"), [social service](/wiki/Social_service "Social service") and [public affairs](/wiki/Public_affairs_(broadcasting) "Public affairs (broadcasting)"). It has also been awarded to some distinguished individuals who were not citizens of India but did contribute in various ways to India.

The selection criteria have been criticised in some quarters with the claim that many highly deserving artists have been left out in order to favour certain individuals.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) India has now created an online nomination platform for the common citizens to recommend the nomination for the annually given civilian "Padma" awards.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

On its obverse, the words "[Padma](/wiki/Padma_(attribute) "Padma (attribute)")", meaning lotus in [Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit "Sanskrit"), and "[Shri](/wiki/Sri "Sri")", a Sanskrit-derived honorific equivalent to 'Mr.' or 'Ms.' (i.e., "Noble One in Blossom"), appear in [Devanagari](/wiki/Devanagari "Devanagari") above and below a [lotus](/wiki/Nelumbo_nucifera "Nelumbo nucifera") flower. The geometrical pattern on either side is in burnished [bronze](/wiki/Bronze "Bronze"). All embossing is in [white gold](/wiki/White_gold "White gold").

As of 2025[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Padma_Shri&action=edit), 3448 people have received the award. In 2025, 113 people received the Padma Shri.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Refusals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Padma_Shri&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Refusals")]

See also: [List of people who have declined or renounced Indian honours and decorations](/wiki/List_of_people_who_have_declined_or_renounced_Indian_honours_and_decorations "List of people who have declined or renounced Indian honours and decorations")

Several intended recipients, including musician [Hemanta Kumar Mukherjee](/wiki/Hemant_Kumar "Hemant Kumar"), [sitar](/wiki/Sitar "Sitar") player [Vilayat Khan](/wiki/Vilayat_Khan "Vilayat Khan"),[[6]](#cite_note-refusals-6) academic and author [Mamoni Raisom Goswami](/wiki/Mamoni_Raisom_Goswami "Mamoni Raisom Goswami"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) journalist Kanak Sen Deka[[8]](#cite_note-question_mark-8) and noted [Bollywood](/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") screenwriter [Salim Khan](/wiki/Salim_Khan "Salim Khan"), have declined the Padma Shri for various reasons.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Some intended recipients, such as environmental activist [Sunderlal Bahuguna](/wiki/Sunderlal_Bahuguna "Sunderlal Bahuguna")[[10]](#cite_note-10) and [English billiards](/wiki/English_billiards "English billiards") champion [Michael Ferreira](/wiki/Michael_Ferreira "Michael Ferreira"),[[8]](#cite_note-question_mark-8) have refused the honour but have subsequently accepted a more prestigious one such as the [Padma Bhushan](/wiki/Padma_Bhushan "Padma Bhushan") or [Padma Vibhushan](/wiki/Padma_Vibhushan "Padma Vibhushan"). Other individuals, such as film-maker [Aribam Syam Sharma](/wiki/Aribam_Syam_Sharma "Aribam Syam Sharma"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) author [Phanishwar Nath 'Renu'](/wiki/Phanishwar_Nath_%27Renu%27 "Phanishwar Nath 'Renu'"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) Punjabi author [Dalip Kaur Tiwana](/wiki/Dalip_Kaur_Tiwana "Dalip Kaur Tiwana")[[13]](#cite_note-13) and noted poet [Jayanta Mahapatra](/wiki/Jayanta_Mahapatra "Jayanta Mahapatra"),[[14]](#cite_note-14) have returned the honour after initially accepting it.

In 2022, Bengali singer "Gitashri" [Sandhya Mukhopadhyay](/wiki/Sandhya_Mukhopadhyay "Sandhya Mukhopadhyay"), aged 90, turned down her offer for the Padma Shri award on the eve of the 73rd Republic Day of India. As per media reports, the veteran singer turned down the offer since she believes that her career spanning eight decades deserved a higher award than the Padma Shri. "Padma Shri is more deserving for a junior artiste", her daughter said.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Based on her refusal, her name was not included in the Padma awardees list for 2022.
